binaries.title: ksrext.sys (Windows Kernel Soft Restart Extension Driver) - KSR persistence-subsystem feature expansion

verified_function_counts.note: The patched build adds roughly 108 new functions (persist/unpersist/query/database API surface). The matcher's unmatched=0 is an address-reuse folding artifact and does not reflect the actual added feature code.

changed_functions.name: KsrPersistMemoryPartition
key_changes: Unpatched acquires SHARED KsrpPdDatabaseLock (ExAcquirePushLockSharedEx @ 0x1C0008856) only to walk KsrpMemoryPartitionList checking list_entry+0x10 == arg2 (0x1C0008872), then releases it with ExReleasePushLockSharedEx (@ 0x1C000888D, matched acquire/release) before the persist work

Patched acquires EXCLUSIVE KsrpPartitionLock (ExAcquirePushLockExclusiveEx @ 0x14000A8EA) and holds it across the check, persist call, and the new KsrpAddPartitionToList insertion, releasing at 0x14000AB32

Patched ADDS ObReferenceObjectByHandle(Handle, 2, PsPartitionType, KernelMode, &Object, NULL) @ 0x14000A98D and performs ObQueryNameString/ObGetObjectSecurity/KsrpQueryMemoryRuns/KsrpQueryIoSpaceRuns on the referenced object, releasing it via ObfDereferenceObjectWithTag @ 0x14000AB64
Unpatched performs the same Ob*/Ksrp* calls on the caller-supplied object pointer (r12), kept alive for the duration by the handle opened at ObOpenObjectByPointerWithTag @ 0x1C00088DA
Patched adds explicit KsrpAddPartitionToList @ 0x14000AB0A (a new helper with no unpatched counterpart) for list insertion under the exclusive lock
Refactoring: ExAllocatePoolWithTag @ 0x1C0008948 -> ExAllocatePool2(0x102,...) @ 0x14000A9FF; indirect->direct call conversions
changed_functions.security_notes: No security-relevant change. The 'handle leak on success' is a false positive: on success the unpatched build transfers the opened handle into the persisted-partition record (var_148 copied to record field var_E8 @ 0x1C0008A01) and hands the record to the persist routine; ObCloseHandle is never referenced in the image (cleanup uses ZwClose @ 0x1C0008A83 on the error path). The suspected use-after-free requires a 'free side' (unpersist) that does not exist in the unpatched build, and the object is referenced by the open handle during the work. Neither build exposes a user-mode attack surface (no IoCreateDevice / IRP_MJ dispatch / IoCreateSymbolicLink). The exclusive lock and explicit ObReferenceObjectByHandle are lifetime/concurrency hardening introduced to support the newly-added persist/unpersist lifecycle, not a remediation of a demonstrable attacker-reachable vulnerability.
changed_functions.assembly_diff:
; KsrPersistMemoryPartition UNPATCHED @ 0x1C0008780
00000001C000884D  lea     rcx, KsrpPdDatabaseLock
00000001C0008856  call    cs:__imp_ExAcquirePushLockSharedEx
00000001C0008872  cmp     [rax+10h], r12          ; list_entry+0x10 == arg2
00000001C0008886  lea     rcx, KsrpPdDatabaseLock
00000001C000888D  call    cs:__imp_ExReleasePushLockSharedEx
00000001C00088DA  call    cs:__imp_ObOpenObjectByPointerWithTag
00000001C000890A  call    cs:__imp_ObQueryNameString
00000001C0008980  call    cs:__imp_ObGetObjectSecurity
00000001C00089A2  call    KsrpQueryMemoryRuns
00000001C00089BD  call    KsrpQueryIoSpaceRuns
00000001C00089E9  mov     rax, [rbp+0E0h+var_148]  ; handle
00000001C0008A01  mov     [rbp+0E0h+var_E8], rax   ; transferred into persisted record
00000001C0008A76  test    ebx, ebx
00000001C0008A78  jns     short loc_1C0008A8F      ; success: handle owned by record
00000001C0008A83  call    cs:__imp_ZwClose         ; error path only
;
; KsrPersistMemoryPartition PATCHED @ 0x14000A820
000000014000A8EA  call    cs:__imp_ExAcquirePushLockExclusiveEx  ; KsrpPartitionLock, held throughout
000000014000A906  cmp     [rax+10h], rbx
000000014000A94F  call    cs:__imp_ObOpenObjectByPointerWithTag
000000014000A98D  call    cs:__imp_ObReferenceObjectByHandle    ; ADDED
000000014000A9C1  call    cs:__imp_ObQueryNameString            ; on referenced object rsi
000000014000AA3E  call    cs:__imp_ObGetObjectSecurity
000000014000AA61  call    KsrpQueryMemoryRuns
000000014000AA7C  call    KsrpQueryIoSpaceRuns
000000014000AB0A  call    KsrpAddPartitionToList                ; ADDED helper
000000014000AB32  call    cs:__imp_ExReleasePushLockExclusiveEx
000000014000AB64  call    cs:__imp_ObfDereferenceObjectWithTag
000000014000AB7A  call    cs:__imp_ZwClose

changed_functions.name: KsrQueryPersistedMemoryPartition
key_changes: Reference counting is present in BOTH builds: unpatched calls ObfReferenceObject @ 0x1C0008C46, patched calls ObfReferenceObject @ 0x14000AF8D

Only difference: lock relaxed from EXCLUSIVE (ExAcquirePushLockExclusiveEx on KsrpPdDatabaseLock @ 0x1C0008BF7) to SHARED (ExAcquirePushLockSharedEx on KsrpPartitionLock @ 0x14000AF0F) for the read-only lookup
changed_functions.security_notes: No security-relevant change and the originally-suspected 'missing ObfReferenceObject' is reversed: both builds already reference-count the returned partition object. The only change is a lock relaxation (exclusive->shared) for read concurrency, which is safe because the returned object is reference-counted in both builds. Correct unpatched address is 0x1C0008BC0 (not 0x1c00097d0).

changed_functions.name: KsrUnpersistMemoryPartition
key_changes: KsrUnpersistMemoryPartition (patched @ 0x14000B450) has NO unpatched counterpart; the unpatched image contains no unpersist function of any kind
The previously-cited unpatched 0x1C000B094 is KsrpRestoreMemoryPartition, an unrelated restore-path routine; the low-similarity match to the patched unpersist is an address-reuse artifact
changed_functions.security_notes: No security-relevant change. This is newly-added feature code. Because the unpersist capability did not exist in the unpatched build, there is no prior code with a lifetime or cleanup defect for the patch to remediate.

changed_functions.name: KsrRegisterLoaderCallbacks
key_changes: Callback-registration structure and initialization/cleanup paths refactored
Indirect calls via global function pointers replaced with direct calls
Relocated conditional/flag globals

changed_functions.name: KsrClaimPersistedMemory
key_changes: ExAllocatePoolWithTag replaced with ExAllocatePool2 (flag 0x102 = PagedPool | Uninitialized, equivalent to prior behavior)
ExFreePoolWithTag replaced with ExFreePool
Indirect calls via global function pointers replaced with direct calls
Relocated flag globals

changed_functions.name: KsrpRestoreMemoryPartition (restore worker family)
key_changes: Heavily restructured; patched adds registry-query functionality
Patched adds NUMA-node-aware thread handling and additional restore worker routines
ExAllocatePool2 replaces ExAllocatePoolWithTag

changed_functions.name: sub_1C000E69C
key_changes: Register allocation changes
ExAllocatePoolWithTag->ExAllocatePool2 migration
Register renames in MDL allocation loop logic

changed_functions.name: sub_1C000F460
key_changes: Argument reordering
Register renames throughout
Indirect calls->direct calls refactoring

changed_functions.name: sub_1C0009B44
key_changes: Register allocation changes in tree traversal
ExAllocatePoolWithTag->ExAllocatePool2
Relocated flag global
Indirect->direct call refactoring

changed_functions.name: KsrMmAllocateNumaPagesForMdlEx
key_changes: Unpatched has inline ZwQuerySystemInformation(0x65,...) for NUMA node detection
Patched delegates to a NUMA-partition wrapper

changed_functions.name: KsrQueryMetadata
key_changes: Restructuring of metadata query logic
Pool allocation migration to ExAllocatePool2

changed_functions.name: KsrFreePersistedMemory / KsrFreePersistedMemoryBlock
key_changes: ExFreePoolWithTag->ExFreePool migration
Indirect->direct call refactoring

changed_functions.name: KsrPdRetrieveMemoryRegion (0x1C000CD34)
key_changes: Patched moves a bounds check inline before array access
The equivalent check exists in the unpatched caller KsrpPdBlockIdToKeyValueEntry (0x1C000CC94), so this is defense-in-depth
changed_functions.security_notes: Not a fix for an exploitable gap: the unpatched caller performs the bounds check before the call. The patched version moves the check inline for defense-in-depth.

changed_functions.name: sub_1C0001C40 / sub_140001FD0
key_changes: Signature simplified
Relocated global flag checks
Indirect->direct call refactoring

changed_functions.name: KsrConnectSecureLoader / KsrInitPageDatabase / KsrCleanupPageDatabase / KsrMdlToMemoryRuns / KsrMmAllocatePagesForMdlEx / KsrMmFreePagesFromMdl
key_changes: Indirect->direct call refactoring
Relocated global data addresses
Register allocation differences from compiler/version change

added: KsrUnpersistMemoryPartition (0x14000B450) and the KsrpAddPartitionToList (0x14000D58C), KsrDbp*, KsrDb*, and KsrPd* persist/unpersist/query families are new in the patched build (roughly 108 added functions). The matcher reported unmatched=0 due to address-reuse folding; these are genuinely new feature functions.

security_findings.entry_point: KSR infrastructure function invoked by the kernel soft-restart/servicing path; not reachable from user mode (no device object, IOCTL dispatch, or symbolic link in either build)
security_findings.vulnerability_class: No security-relevant change (originally suspected CWE-367 / CWE-911 / CWE-404)
security_findings.description: Originally reported as a TOCTOU race plus missing object reference plus handle leak. Against the binaries none of these hold. (1) Locking: the unpatched build uses a SHARED lock for the list-presence check and releases it with the matching ExReleasePushLockSharedEx (0x1C000888D) before the persist work; the patched build uses an EXCLUSIVE lock held throughout. This is stricter locking introduced to support the newly-added unpersist lifecycle, but the 'free side' (unpersist) does not exist in the unpatched build, so no reachable use-after-free is present there. (2) Object reference: the patched build adds ObReferenceObjectByHandle (0x14000A98D); the unpatched build performs the same operations on the caller-supplied pointer, which is kept alive by the handle opened at ObOpenObjectByPointerWithTag. (3) Handle leak: false positive. On success the unpatched build stores the opened handle into the persisted-partition record (var_148 -> var_E8 at 0x1C0008A01) and hands the record to the persist routine; the handle is transferred, not leaked, and is closed via ZwClose only on the error path (0x1C0008A83). ObCloseHandle is not referenced anywhere in the image.
security_findings.trigger_conditions: None demonstrable. Neither build exposes a user-mode attack surface, and the unpatched build has no unpersist path to free a persisted object concurrently.
security_findings.primitive: None. No memory-corruption, information-disclosure, or denial-of-service primitive is supported by the binaries.
security_findings.evidence: Unpatched @ 0x1C0008780: ExAcquirePushLockSharedEx (0x1C0008856) on KsrpPdDatabaseLock; list check cmp [rax+10h], r12 (0x1C0008872); ExReleasePushLockSharedEx (0x1C000888D); ObOpenObjectByPointerWithTag (0x1C00088DA); handle stored into record var_148->var_E8 (0x1C0008A01); jns skips ZwClose on success (0x1C0008A78), ZwClose only on error (0x1C0008A83). Patched @ 0x14000A820: ExAcquirePushLockExclusiveEx (0x14000A8EA); ObReferenceObjectByHandle (0x14000A98D); KsrpAddPartitionToList (0x14000AB0A); ExReleasePushLockExclusiveEx (0x14000AB32); ObfDereferenceObjectWithTag (0x14000AB64).
security_findings.assembly:
; UNPATCHED KsrPersistMemoryPartition @ 0x1C0008780
00000001C000884D  lea     rcx, KsrpPdDatabaseLock
00000001C0008856  call    cs:__imp_ExAcquirePushLockSharedEx
00000001C0008872  cmp     [rax+10h], r12
00000001C000888D  call    cs:__imp_ExReleasePushLockSharedEx
00000001C00088DA  call    cs:__imp_ObOpenObjectByPointerWithTag
00000001C000890A  call    cs:__imp_ObQueryNameString
00000001C0008980  call    cs:__imp_ObGetObjectSecurity
00000001C00089A2  call    KsrpQueryMemoryRuns
00000001C00089BD  call    KsrpQueryIoSpaceRuns
00000001C00089E9  mov     rax, [rbp+0E0h+var_148]
00000001C0008A01  mov     [rbp+0E0h+var_E8], rax
00000001C0008A76  test    ebx, ebx
00000001C0008A78  jns     short loc_1C0008A8F
00000001C0008A83  call    cs:__imp_ZwClose

breakpoints.why: Entry of the persist function; rdx = arg2 = partition object pointer.
ExAcquirePushLockSharedEx on KsrpPdDatabaseLock; shared lock for the list-presence check only.
ExReleasePushLockSharedEx; matched release of the shared lock.
The opened handle (var_148) is copied into the persisted record field (var_E8), showing ownership transfer rather than a leak.

watch: rdx at entry = partition object pointer (arg2)
r12 at 0x1C0008872 = arg2 compared against list_entry+0x10
ebx at 0x1C0008A76 = NTSTATUS; jns at 0x1C0008A78 skips ZwClose on success because the handle is owned by the record
key_offsets: 0x1C0008A01: handle transferred into persisted record (var_148 -> var_E8)
debugging_aids.trigger_setup: No local trigger. Neither build exposes a user-mode entry point (no IoCreateDevice / IRP_MJ dispatch / IoCreateSymbolicLink), and the unpatched build has no unpersist path.
debugging_aids.notes: ksrext.sys is the Windows Kernel Soft Restart extension driver. The persist/query APIs are invoked by the kernel's soft-restart/servicing infrastructure. Addresses are relative to the unpatched preferred base 0x1C0000000 and the patched preferred base 0x140000000.

security_findings.entry_point: KSR infrastructure function; not user-mode reachable
security_findings.vulnerability_class: No security-relevant change (originally suspected CWE-911 / CWE-416, reversed)
security_findings.description: The originally-reported 'missing ObfReferenceObject' is reversed against the binaries: the unpatched build already calls ObfReferenceObject (0x1C0008C46) on the located partition object before returning it, exactly as the patched build does (0x14000AF8D). The only difference is that the lock is relaxed from EXCLUSIVE (unpatched, 0x1C0008BF7) to SHARED (patched, 0x14000AF0F). The relaxation is safe because the returned object is reference-counted in both builds. Correct unpatched address is 0x1C0008BC0.
security_findings.trigger_conditions: None; no reference was removed and no lifetime bug exists.
security_findings.evidence: Unpatched 0x1C0008C46 ObfReferenceObject; patched 0x14000AF8D ObfReferenceObject. Lock: unpatched ExAcquirePushLockExclusiveEx 0x1C0008BF7 vs patched ExAcquirePushLockSharedEx 0x14000AF0F.

breakpoints.why: Entry of the query function. ObfReferenceObject is called on the located object at 0x1C0008C46 before it is returned (present in both builds).

watch: rbx after list match = located partition object; ObfReferenceObject increments its reference in both builds
debugging_aids.notes: Both builds reference-count the returned object; only the lock strength changed (exclusive -> shared).

security_findings.entry_point: New function in the patched build (0x14000B450); no unpatched counterpart
security_findings.vulnerability_class: No security-relevant change (new feature; original CWE-404 / CWE-367 attribution was to the wrong function)
security_findings.description: The unpatched image contains no unpersist function. The previously-cited unpatched address 0x1C000B094 is KsrpRestoreMemoryPartition, an unrelated restore-path routine; the low-similarity match to the patched unpersist is an address-reuse artifact. KsrUnpersistMemoryPartition is newly-added feature code, so there is no prior vulnerable code to remediate.
security_findings.evidence: Unpatched 0x1C000B094 header is 'KsrpRestoreMemoryPartition'; no 'Unpersist' function exists in the unpatched image. Patched 'KsrUnpersistMemoryPartition' @ 0x14000B450.

breakpoints.why: Newly-added unpersist function in the patched build; there is nothing to compare against in the unpatched build.

debugging_aids.notes: 0x1C000B094 in the unpatched build is KsrpRestoreMemoryPartition, not an unpersist function.

summary: No security-relevant change. The patch is a feature expansion of the Kernel Soft Restart (KSR) memory-partition persistence subsystem (unpatched 151 functions -> patched 259; roughly 108 added functions) plus pool-API modernization (ExAllocatePoolWithTag -> ExAllocatePool2). Neither build exposes a user-mode attack surface (no device object, IOCTL dispatch, or symbolic link). The three originally-suspected findings do not hold against the binaries: (1) KsrPersistMemoryPartition's shared->exclusive lock and added ObReferenceObjectByHandle are lifetime/concurrency hardening introduced to support the newly-added unpersist lifecycle, and the reported 'handle leak' is a verified ownership transfer of the handle into the persisted-partition record (closed via ZwClose only on the error path); (2) KsrQueryPersistedMemoryPartition already calls ObfReferenceObject in both builds, with only a lock relaxation (exclusive->shared); (3) KsrUnpersistMemoryPartition is new feature code with no unpatched counterpart (the cited 0x1C000B094 is KsrpRestoreMemoryPartition). No attacker-reachable memory-corruption, information-disclosure, or denial-of-service primitive is present or fixed.
